pi_items.source: Task description in task.json specifies this exact privilege name to create. Setup script checks for and cleans up this privilege by name. Export script queries API for privilege 'View Triage Queue'.
Task description states: 'Allows users to view the patient triage queue and priority levels' - contains both 'triage' and 'queue' keywords. Verifier checks description against these keywords.
Hardcoded in task_utils.sh and setup_bahmni.sh as BAHMNI_ADMIN_USERNAME. Web search confirms this is the standard default Bahmni demo username documented in official Bahmni community forums.

Hardcoded in task_utils.sh and setup_bahmni.sh as BAHMNI_ADMIN_PASSWORD. Web search confirms this is the standard default Bahmni demo password documented in official Bahmni forums and configuration guides.

env.json shows Bahmni Lite runs in Docker containers. setup_bahmni.sh uses docker-compose to spin up containerized OpenMRS with MySQL. seed_bahmni.py creates synthetic patient data with procedurally generated names based on Census Bureau public demographic data.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a custom privilege named 'View Triage Queue' with a description containing the keywords 'triage' and 'queue' in a synthetic Bahmni/OpenMRS environment. The environment uses standard demo credentials (username: superman, password: Admin123) that are publicly documented in Bahmni official forums and guides. Patient data is procedurally generated with synthetic names and UUIDs. The OpenMRS instance runs locally at https://localhost with self-signed SSL certificate. No real hospital data or non-standard credentials are involved.
